213.reason: [Error: Should create a token with type representing single '&' (not AND), and this will cause incorrect error reporting later. The token type should not be AND when it's a single '&'.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Should create a token with type representing single '&' (not AND), and this will cause incorrect error reporting later. The token type should not be AND when it's a single '&'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Single '&' should not be treated as AND token type. It should either be flagged as an invalid token or handled differently, but creating an AND token with incorrect content "&&" for a single '&' character is wrong. This will cause the parser to see an AND token instead of recognizing it as an invalid single '&' which should trigger error category 'a'.]

224.reason: [When encountering a single '&' or '|' character, the code incorrectly creates an AND/OR token with content "&&"/"||" instead of using the actual single character "&"/"|". This causes incorrect error reporting because the token content doesn't match the actual input character, and the error type 'a' should be generated during syntax analysis but the wrong token type and content will confuse the parser.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Same issue as above - single '|' should not be represented as OR token type. This misrepresentation will prevent proper detection of error type 'a'.]

This causes incorrect error reporting because the token content doesn't match the actual input character, and the error type 'a' should be generated during syntax analysis but the wrong token type and content will confuse the parser.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Single '|' should not be treated as OR token type. Similar to the '&' case, creating an OR token with content "||" for a single '|' character is incorrect. This prevents proper detection of error category 'a' for single '|' characters.]
